name: Michigan United
description_short: Michigan United Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured credit union formed in 2021 from a merger of four Michigan-based credit unions, offering savings accounts, certificates, auto loans, and home equity products.
description_long:
Michigan United Credit Union was established on July 1, 2021, following the merger of four small-asset credit unions: Birmingham Bloomfield Credit Union, Metro North Federal Credit Union, Lakes Community Credit Union, and Unity Credit Union. The organization traces its roots back to November 23, 1931, when Birmingham Teachers Credit Union was chartered to serve educators in the Birmingham School system. This long history of community-focused financial service provided the foundation for the modern unified institution serving Michigan residents today.

Michigan United offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including share savings accounts (basic savings), share certificates with APYs up to 3.96%, IRA certificates, Super Saver accounts, auto loans from 4.05% APR, home equity lines of credit from 5.74% APR, and used vehicle loans from 6.49% APR. Members can access online banking, mobile check deposit, fund transfers, balance inquiries, and shared branching through over 4,500 CO-OP branches and 150 Xtend locations nationally. The credit union maintains 10 physical branch locations across Michigan in Auburn Hills, Birmingham, Holly, Lake Orion, Shelby Township, St. Clair Shores, Warren, and Waterford.

Michigan United distinguishes itself through its member-owned, not-for-profit structure that prioritizes member benefit over shareholder profit. The organization emphasizes community involvement and has expanded its service footprint and product offerings significantly through strategic consolidation. Its mission explicitly focuses on enriching members' financial lives and supporting Michigan communities. The credit union provides no-surcharge ATM access and shares branch networks, giving members access far beyond its 10 physical locations.

A primary consideration is that membership eligibility requires residence, employment, worship, or school attendance in Michigan. While rates are competitive, the "as low as" pricing structure means individual rates depend on creditworthiness. The credit union is still relatively new in its current form (2021 merger), though the component institutions have substantial history. Members should verify current rates and specific loan terms, as these are subject to change. The organization is properly federally insured by the NCUA, providing standard deposit protection.
services: Share savings accounts (basic savings with $5 minimum)
Share certificates with rates up to 3.96% APY
Home equity lines of credit from 5.74% APR
Used vehicle loans for 2015 and older vehicles from 6.49% APR
Shared branching access (4,500+ CO-OP branches and 150+ Xtend locations)
ATM locator and no-surcharge ATM network access
Check ordering, wire transfers, and bill payment services
pros: NCUA federal insurance protects deposits up to $250,000 per account category
Access to 4,500+ CO-OP shared branches and 150+ Xtend locations nationally beyond 10 local branches
Competitive rates on certificates (up to 3.96% APY) and auto loans (from 4.05% APR) as of April 2026
Low membership requirement: only $5 minimum deposit to open a share account and maintain membership
Michigan-wide membership eligibility for those who reside, work, worship, or attend school in the state
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means earnings benefit members rather than external shareholders
No-surcharge ATM network access and online banking with mobile check deposit functionality
Established community roots dating back to 1931 with proven track record through multiple predecessor institutions
cons: Membership restricted to Michigan residents, employees, students, or those who worship in the state—no national membership
Super Saver account rates very low at only 0.25% APY, offering minimal returns on savings
Rates are variable and subject to change without notice; advertised rates are conditional on credit history
Only 10 physical branch locations across Michigan, limiting in-person service accessibility for some members
Used vehicle loans only available for 2015 and older vehicles, excluding newer car financing options
best_for: Michigan residents and employees seeking relationship banking with a member-owned, community-focused institution
Borrowers with good-to-excellent credit looking for competitive auto loan and home equity rates
Members who value access to shared branching networks and need flexibility beyond local branch locations
Long-term savers interested in certificate products and savings accounts with NCUA federal insurance protection
diagnosis: Michigan United Credit Union is best for Michigan-based consumers seeking competitive deposit rates, auto financing, and home equity products from a federally insured, member-owned institution with extensive shared branching access. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to those with Michigan ties (residence, employment, worship, or school attendance), making it unavailable to out-of-state consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Michigan United — NCUA-insured credit union in Auburn Hills, MI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
